name: Sentier des Mélèzes Loop
description: A challenging 8.5-mile (13.8 km) jogging loop in the Vallée du Louron, offering significant elevation gain and panoramic views of Lake Génos
region_intro: Jogging routes around Génos are set within the historic Louron Valley, offering diverse terrain for runners. The region is characterized by the majestic Pyrenees mountains and the central Génos-Loudenvielle Lake, providing a scenic backdrop for outdoor activity. Runners can find paths winding through forests and along the lake, with varied elevation profiles. This area presents a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging running trails. Best jogging routes around Génos The most popular jogging route is Lake Génos – Panorama of the valley loop from Génos, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the valley. Another top favourite among local runners is Tour of lake Loudenvielle, a difficult 8.5 miles (13.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain around the lake, providing a challenging experience. Local runners also love the View of Lake Loudenvielle loop from Génos, a 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the lake, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Jogging around Génos is defined by the Pyrenees mountains, the Louron Valley, and the Génos-Loudenvielle Lake. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy lakeside paths to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Génos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 5500 runners have used komoot to explore Génos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Génos?
A: Génos offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 trails mapped on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy lakeside paths to challenging mountain runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Génos?
A: The jogging trails in Génos feature diverse terrain, from mostly paved surfaces around the Génos-Loudenvielle Lake to paths winding through forests and offering views of the surrounding Pyrenees mountains. You'll find a mix of flat sections and significant elevation gains, depending on the route chosen.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Génos?
A: Yes, for those seeking an easier run, the 2.2-mile (5,000-step) route around the Génos-Loudenvielle Lake is ideal. It features mostly paved surfaces and minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for all fitness levels and a great option for families.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails with significant elevation in Génos?
A: Absolutely. For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879129520/tour-of-lake-loudenvielle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of lake Loudenvielle&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 13.8 km route features significant elevation gain around the lake, providing a strenuous workout with rewarding views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40052083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos – Panorama of the valley loop from Génos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route. This 8.8 km trail takes about an hour to complete and offers stunning panoramic views of the Louron Valley.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while running in Génos?
A: While running, you can encounter several interesting attractions. The historic watchtowers of Génos and Moulor, dating back to the 13th and 12th centuries respectively, stand prominently on the heights surrounding the lake. The picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4815138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos-Loudenvielle&lt;/a&gt; itself is a central feature, and you might also spot other beautiful lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5186545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Oô&lt;/a&gt; in the broader region.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Génos?
A: The running routes in Génos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views of the Pyrenees and the lake, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for endurance training?
A: For longer runs, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6981775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Loudenvielle loop from Génos&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This difficult 18.2 km trail leads through varied terrain with continuous views of the lake, typically completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Génos?
A: Génos is appealing for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While the region transforms in winter with proximity to ski resorts, some trails around the lake remain accessible, offering snowy vistas. Summer is also popular, but it's advisable to run during cooler parts of the day.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty routes that explore the area beyond the immediate lake shore?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15016103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos-Loudenvielle – Lake Génos loop from Pène des Aubères&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.3 km route. It offers a great way to explore the area around the lake with some elevation, providing a good balance of challenge and scenic beauty.

Q: Can I find routes that combine lake views with more extensive climbs?
A: Certainly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20532632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos-Loudenvielle – Loudenvielle Lake loop from Génos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.4 km route that provides extensive lake views while incorporating significant elevation changes, perfect for runners looking for a demanding yet scenic experience.

Q: Are there options for relaxation and recovery after a run in Génos?
A: Yes, after an invigorating run, you can find relaxation at the nearby Balnéa spa, the largest thermal spa in the French Pyrenees. It offers pools, hammams, saunas, and massages, providing a perfect way to recover and unwind.
tips:
Superb winding path with viewpoints over the Louron valley and Lac de Génos, passing from the forest to the clear path on the mountainside.
It is also a trail route; very nice downhill race!!
A nice view of this lake (it's a dam lake) for a sandwich break before getting back on the road!

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead for the Sentier des Mélèzes Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop is accessible from Loudenvielle. While specific parking details aren't provided, a shuttle service is available, suggesting public transport options or designated drop-off points in the area.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and difficulty like for jogging on this loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered difficult and is best suited for experienced joggers or trail runners. The terrain is varied, featuring winding paths through forested sections and open mountainsides. Expect significant elevation changes, including a steep 2.5 km climb with a 450-meter gain, which is part of the "Verticale du Louron" route. There's also a very enjoyable single-track descent through the forest.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Joggers can enjoy magnificent panoramic views of the Louron valley, its high peaks, and Lake Génos once on the ridges. The trail also passes through intimate settings within the state forest of Neste du Louron, featuring beautiful specimens of Douglas firs and larch trees. A key highlight is the View of Lake Loudenvielle.

faqs.question: Is the Sentier des Mélèzes Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed, trails in the Pyrénées generally welcome dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash, especially in protected areas or during certain seasons. It's always best to check local signage at the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation and mountain environment, the best time to jog this loop is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October) when the weather is more stable and trails are clear of snow. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for jogging the Sentier des Mélèzes Loop itself. However, as it's located in the Vallée du Louron, general regulations for mountain areas should be observed.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sentier des Mélèzes 15, La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.5, Val d'Aube 8, Sentier des Mélèzes, Sentier du Pla de Nabias au Bois de Playstrou, Sentier des Sources de Lias, Sentier des Granges de Cider, Chemin de Plaistrou, and Chemin de la Forge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sentier des Mélèzes Loop by jogging?
faqs.answer: For an average jogger, this 13.76 km loop with significant elevation gain typically takes around 2 hours and 18 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What kind of natural environment can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse natural environment, transitioning between dense forested sections with beautiful Douglas firs and larch trees, and open mountainsides. You'll experience noticeable shifts in atmosphere between shaded and sunny areas, providing a dynamic jogging experience.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety warnings or tips for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Due to its challenging nature and potentially technical sections, it's important to stay on the marked trail. Be prepared for steep ascents and descents, and wear appropriate trail running footwear. Always carry enough water and check weather forecasts before you go, as conditions in the mountains can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginner runners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is classified as difficult and is not recommended for beginner runners. The significant elevation changes, including a steep climb, and varied terrain make it more suitable for experienced joggers or trail runners looking for a challenging workout.
